result.preview: "I\u2019m unable to accurately restrict the clinical cohort to BRCA (breast cancer) patients who are alive because the clinical_info table in the clinical_database does not expose a clear cancer-type field such as Cancer_type_acronym, nor a straightforward vital-status field, and these columns cannot be inferred reliably from the available schema. Without a way to (1) select only BRCA patients and (2) identify which of them are alive, I cannot validly calculate histological-type\u2013specific CDH1 mutation percentages for the requested subgroup.\n\nGiven these limitations of the provided databases, I\u2019m not able to determine which top three histological types among alive BRCA patients in the PanCancer Atlas show the highest percentage of CDH1 gene mutations."

args.answer:
I’m unable to accurately restrict the clinical cohort to BRCA (breast cancer) patients who are alive because the clinical_info table in the clinical_database does not expose a clear cancer-type field such as Cancer_type_acronym, nor a straightforward vital-status field, and these columns cannot be inferred reliably from the available schema. Without a way to (1) select only BRCA patients and (2) identify which of them are alive, I cannot validly calculate histological-type–specific CDH1 mutation percentages for the requested subgroup.

Given these limitations of the provided databases, I’m not able to determine which top three histological types among alive BRCA patients in the PanCancer Atlas show the highest percentage of CDH1 gene mutations.

val_args.answer:
I’m unable to accurately restrict the clinical cohort to BRCA (breast cancer) patients who are alive because the clinical_info table in the clinical_database does not expose a clear cancer-type field such as Cancer_type_acronym, nor a straightforward vital-status field, and these columns cannot be inferred reliably from the available schema. Without a way to (1) select only BRCA patients and (2) identify which of them are alive, I cannot validly calculate histological-type–specific CDH1 mutation percentages for the requested subgroup.

Given these limitations of the provided databases, I’m not able to determine which top three histological types among alive BRCA patients in the PanCancer Atlas show the highest percentage of CDH1 gene mutations.
